    N: Download is performed unsandboxed as root, as file '/home/user/Downloads/docker-desktop.deb' couldn't be accessed by user '_apt'. - pkgAcquire::Run (13: Permission denied)

    Existem algumas etapas de configuração pós-instalação feitas através do script pós-instalação contido no pacote deb, são elas:

    • Definir a capacidade no binário do Docker Desktop para mapear portas privilegiadas e definir limites de recursos;
    • Criar um link de /usr/bin/docker para /usr/local/bin/com.docker.cli;
    • Adicionar um DNS (Sistema de Nomes de Domínio) para o Kubernetes em /etc/hosts (Explicarei em um artigo posterior a esse o que é Kubernetes).

    Pronto, o Docker Desktop está instalado, vamos agora iniciar ele pela primeira. :-D

    Iniciando o Docker Desktop

    Siga o seguintes passos para o sucesso e torça para que no final tudo ocorreu bem e você seja muito feliz com sua primeira instalcão do Docker Desktop:

    1. Abra o menu Applications (Aplicativos) no Gnome/KDE Desktop e procure pelo Docker Desktop.

    2. Selecione Docker Desktop para iniciar o Docker. Será exibido o Docker Subscription Service Agreement.

    3. Marque a caixa de seleção para aceitar os termos atualizados e clique em Accept (Aceito) para continuar.
      Importante
      Se você não concordar com os termos, o aplicativo Docker Desktop será fechado e você não poderá mais executar o Docker Desktop em sua máquina. Você pode optar por aceitar os termos posteriormente abrindo o Docker Desktop.

    Se ocorreu tudo bem depois de você aceitar os termos, o Docker Desktop vai iniciar um Quick Start Guide (guia de ínicio rápido). Este guia é um tutorial para criar uma imagem de exemplo do Docker, executá-la como um contêiner, enviar e salvar no Docker Hub. O Docker Hub é uma biblioteca para imagens de contêiner.

    Caso queira permitir que o Docker Desktop seja iniciado no login, no menu Docker, selecione Settings (Configurações) > General (Geral) > Start Docker Desktop when you log in (Inicio o Docker Desktop quando você logar).

    E então chegamos ao fim, ufa! Espero que você tenha gostado e estarei a disposição para tirar as dúvidas que aparecer, só deixar um comentário.

    Bons momentos :-)
item.author: angelobms

item.title: M-One 个人助理 v2.0 - 更新：2022 年 6 月 11 日，星期六 | M-One Personal Assistant v2.0 - Update: Saturday, June 11, 2022
item.description:
大家好，

播放音乐、播放视频、打开照片、做笔记和阅读、在地图上查找位置、whois 站点、天气、检查您是否连接到互联网、在 GitHub 中打开应用程序的源代码以及... 更多功能-改进的用户界面、错误修复和许多其他问题。

核心依赖

操作系统

了解有关 M-One 的更多信息： https ://dev.to/mhadi2003/m-one-personal-assistant-v10-l0h

将来，该程序的一些功能将被添加，它的一些缺点将被消除。
各位亲也可以发表意见和建议。



原文:

Hello guys,

The M-One was built on Sunday, May 8, 2022, and now has its first update on Saturday, June 11, 2022.

Version 2.0 (Update: Saturday, June 11, 2022):

M-One in version 2.0 can do 68 things.

Important changes in version 2.0:

Play music, play videos, open photos, take notes and read them, find locations on the map, whois site, weather, check if you are connected to the Internet, open the source code of the application in GitHub and ... More Features -Improved user interface, bug fixes and lots of other problems.

Core Dependencies

Python 3 (3.9.7) SpeechRecognition subprocess (version 2.0) webbrowser wikipedia pyautogui (version 2.0) translate requests pyqrcode platform (version 2.0) winshell (version 2.0) datetime PyAudio pyttsx3 random (version 2.0) urllib whois (version 2.0) time

os

Project at GitHub: https://github.com/Mhadi-1382/M-One-personal-assistant

Learn more about M-One: https://dev.to/mhadi2003/m-one-personal-assistant-v10-l0h

In the future, some features of this program will be added and some of its shortcomings will be eliminated.
You dear ones can also express your opinions and suggestions.
item.author: mhadi2003

item.title: 这种所有权练习能否改善您与他人的合作方式？ | Can this ownership exercise improve how you work with others?
item.description:
当你在一个小组中工作时，你需要知道如何协调。 协调需要对每个人将如何一起工作达成共识。 大多数人类群体定义角色。 角色有助于澄清人们一起工作的界限和期望。

例如，在体育运动中，这些角色称为职位。 要想打得好，你需要所有的位置来了解如何相互配合。 你需要每个人都专注于他们的立场。 在足球（又称足球）中让每个人都尝试成为守门员是荒谬的。

人们常常不能很好地合作，因为他们缺乏对每个人所扮演角色的共同理解。

多年来，我想出了一个简单的方法来调整人们的角色。 它比诸如 RACI 图表之类的替代方法要简单得多（但我们也会对其进行描述）。 当不清楚谁负责什么时，您可以使用此练习。 您还可以使用它为团队定义团队所有权的边界。

为所有权练习做好准备

我将假设我们在工程经理和产品经理之间使用这个练习。 但是您可以将它用于任何角色。

您首先需要一块白板。 如果您不在现场，您可以使用 Google Doc、Miro、Notion 或 Trello 进行操作。 真的，大多数工具都可以工作。

首先在白板上创建三列：

  1. 在左侧的第一列中写下“显然是工程经理”。
  2. 在右边的第三列写“显然是产品经理”。
  3. 在两者之间，写“不清楚”

进行所有权练习

接下来，您让相关人员为他们的所有职责写下便利贴。 每粘一个。 为什么是胶粘剂？ 您希望能够轻松地在列之间移动内容。 如果您使用的是 Trello 之类的工具，您应该能够在列之间拖动，这样也可以。

您可以通过以下两种方式执行此操作：

  1. 让每个人制作一堆便笺，然后在贴出它们时逐一讨论。
  2. 或者，您可以让人们在创建它们时将它们放置起来。 然后审查以确保每个人都同意他们在你认为他们应该在的地方。

我通常使用第二种方法，因为它更省时。 基本上，您有任何分歧或想要讨论的任何内容都应移至中间栏。

这是添加一些职责后它的外观的混乱版本。 在这个例子中，工程经理和产品经理都认为他们应该做项目报告。 他们都曾在他们的角色负责为团队定义用户故事之前工作过。

仅仅做这个初步的练习就已经很有价值了。 你已经就你的角色达成了很多一致，并且你已经明确了你仍然需要进行哪些对话。

如果您在白板上进行此操作，请拍照。 你稍后会需要它。

讨论你的角色中不清楚的部分

接下来，您要浏览不清楚列中的每个项目。

您可能不会在第一次会议中涵盖所有这些内容。 没关系。 把剩下的时间花在尽可能多的计算上。 这是做什么：

  1. 找出权衡取舍。 对于每项责任，问问自己：对于工程经理负责的最佳论据是什么？ 对于拥有这个的产品经理来说，最好的论据是什么？
  2. 讨论权衡。 讨论你看到的任何顾虑或权衡。
  3. 做出决定，哪怕是暂时的。 如果你不能达成协议，掷硬币并同意在两周后讨论它是如何工作的。

尽可能多地处理不明确的项目，并安排后续会议以完成剩余的任何事情。

记录你学到的东西

您现在已经完成了定义角色的工作。 你们中的一个人现在需要写下你已经同意的内容。 只需列出角色，以及每个角色下的责任范围。

如果您在讨论中提出了一些更大的主题，您还可以对事物进行分类。 例如，您可能会说工程经理负责人员、流程和项目。 但包含示例总是有用的。 较大的主题是可选的。

当团队经历有丝分裂时，这也很方便

当团队变得足够大以至于分成两半时，您可以采用完全相同的方法。 团队将拥有很多现有的东西：部分代码库，以及与组织其他部分的交互。 在练习中运行这些，你会得到一个明确拥有的东西的清单。

对于团队，您通常会在“不清楚”列中有一些粘性项目。 其中一些可能需要技术工作或项目来解开。 例如，您可能拥有两个新团队都将依赖的一段代码。

记录您同意的内容，并就您将在 Unclear 项目上取得进展的一些短期方式达成一致。 并确保您有真正的计划来弄清楚如何处理 Unclear 项目。

在规范的位置写下角色和团队描述

假设您是一名产品经理，并且您刚刚与您的工程经理同行一起完成了这个练习。

你应该问自己的一个问题是，“这样的事情是否存在于其他人身上？”。 如果您没有写下角色定义，那么您提出的工件可能通常很有用。

如果是这种情况，请分享它或将其放在可以充当您角色的规范定义的地方。

对于团队，您还希望将职责放在他们的规范位置。 对于代码，您可以将其放在 CODEOWNERS 文件中，因此在代码库中会清楚地标记它。 对于支持职责，您应该概述支持应如何与每个团队互动。 如果有意义的话，请确保将您产生的清晰度放在其他人可以使用的地方。

人是不可替代的，所以角色需要一些灵活性

角色不应该是僵化的。 每个人都是不同的，每一对人都会有不同的优势、背景和合作方式。

确定一个角色拥有一个责任领域，但将该责任委托给另一个角色应该是一个有效的选择。 例如，工程可以负责项目，但完全埋在其他工作中。 所以你可以同意让产品经理运行一段时间的项目。

此外，如果您有标准的角色定义，并且想要改变你们之间的界限，那么如果它产生更好的结果，您应该这样做。 我发现写下角色可以让你就这些事情进行更明确的对话。 你对我做报道或帮助你有什么感觉？”

有些人喜欢 RACI 图表

您会发现很多经理出于同样的目的而喜欢RACI 图表。 我发现它们难以理解——它们需要很多注意力（对我来说）才能理解。

因此，例如，他们明确了哪些人需要了解某些类型的更改。

当您与很多聚会打交道时，我通常发现它们更有效。 利益相关者和多人，都在做类似的工作。 我在这里分享的所有权练习更简单、更快、更容易，因为涉及的各方较少。

如果您赞成 RACI 图表，请注意并非每个人都能直观地理解它们。 浏览它们可能很有用。

让我知道你的想法！

我发现这个练习是一种快速了解角色并保持一致的方法。 试试看，让我知道你的想法！

这篇文章（带有附加图片）可在https://www.rubick.com/role-definition/获得

谢谢

图片由英国 Black Tech提供。 根据知识共享 2.0 许可获得许可。 图像被裁剪。



原文:

When you’re working in a group, you need to know how to coordinate. Coordination requires a shared understanding of how everyone will work together. Most human groups define Roles. Roles help clarify boundaries and expectations for people working together.

For example, in sports, these roles are called Positions. To play well, you need all the positions to understand how to work with each other. And you need everyone to focus on their position. It would be ridiculous in football (aka soccer) to have everyone try and be the goalkeeper.

People often fail to work well with each other, because they lack that shared understanding of the Roles each person plays.

Over the years, I’ve come up with a simple way to align people on roles. It’s way simpler than alternatives like RACI charts (but we’ll describe those too). You can use this exercise when it isn’t clear who is responsible for what. You can also use it for teams to define boundaries of team ownership.

Getting set up for the ownership exercise

I’m going to assume we’re using this exercise between an Engineering Manager and a Product Manager. But you can use it for any roles.

You’ll first need a whiteboard. If you’re not in person, you can do it with Google Doc, Miro, Notion, or Trello. Really most tools will work.

Start out by creating three columns on the whiteboard:

  1. Write “Clearly Engineering Manager” as the first column, on the left.
  2. Write “Clearly Product Manager” as the third column, on the right.
  3. In between the two, write “Unclear”

Doing the ownership exercise

Next, you have the people involved write down stickies for all their responsibilities. One per sticky. Why stickies? You want to easily be able to move things between columns. If you’re using a tool like Trello, you should be able to drag between columns, so that works as well.

You can do this two ways:

  1. Have each person create a bunch of stickies, and then talk through each as you put them up.
  2. Or, you can have people put them up as they create them. Then review to make sure everyone agrees they are where you think they should be.

I usually use the second approach, as it is a little more time-efficient. Basically anything you have ANY disagreement or want to discuss should be moved to the middle column.

Here’s a messy version of how it might look after adding some responsibilities. In this example, both the Engineering Manager and Product Manager think they should be doing project reporting. And they’ve both worked in places before where their role was responsible for defining user stories for the team.

Just doing this initial exercise has already been valuable. You’ve aligned on a lot about your roles, and you’ve made it explicit what conversations you still need to have.

If you did this on a whiteboard, take a picture. You’ll need it later.

Discuss the unclear parts of your roles

Next, you want to go through each of the items in the unclear column.

You may not cover all of them within this first meeting. That’s fine. Spend the rest of the time you have figuring out as many as you can. Here’s what to do:

  1. Figure out the tradeoffs. For each responsibility, ask yourselves: what’s the best argument for the Engineering Manager being responsible for this? And what’s the best argument for the Product Manager owning this?
  2. Discuss the tradeoffs. Talk through any concerns or tradeoffs you see.
  3. Make a decision, even a temporary one. If you can’t come to agreement, flip a coin and agree to discuss how it’s working after two weeks.

Get through as many Unclear items as you can, and schedule followup meetings to finish up anything remaining.

Document what you’ve learned

You’ve now done the work defining the roles. One of you now needs to write down what you’ve agreed to. Simply list the roles, and the areas of responsibility under each.

You can also categorize things, if you’ve had a discussion where you’ve come up with some larger themes. For example, you might say the Engineering Manager is responsible for People, Process, Projects. But it’s always useful to include examples. The larger themes are optional.

This is also handy when teams go through mitosis

You can follow this exact same approach when a team gets large enough that it splits in half. (Splitting a team in half is a great way to form new teams).

The team will have a lot of existing things it owns: parts of the codebase, and interactions with other parts of the organization. Run these through the exercise, and you’ll have a list of things that are clearly owned.

With teams, you’ll often have some sticky items in the Unclear column. Some of them might require technical work or projects to untangle. For example, you might have a piece of code that both new teams will rely on.

Document what you agree upon, and agree on some short-term ways you’ll move forward on the Unclear items. And make sure you have real plans in place to figure out what to do with the Unclear items.

Write roles and team descriptions down in a canonical location

Let’s say you’re a Product Manager, and you’ve just gone through this exercise with your Engineering Manager counterpart.

A question you should ask yourself is, “does anything like this exist for everyone else?“. If you don’t have role definition written up, then the artifact you came up with might be generally useful.

If that’s the case, share it or put it in a place where it can act as the canonical definition for your roles.

For teams, you also want the responsibilities to be put in their canonical location. For code, you might put it in the CODEOWNERS file, so it’s clearly marked in the codebase. For support responsibilities, you should outline how support should interact with each team. Make sure the clarity you’ve produced is put into a place where it can be used by others, if that makes sense.

People aren’t fungible, so roles need some flexibility

Roles shouldn’t be rigid. Every person is different, and every pair of people will have different strengths, backgrounds, and ways of working together.

It should be a valid option to identify that one role owns an area of responsibility, but delegates that responsibility to the other role. For example, Engineering can be responsible for projects, but be totally buried in other work. So you can agree to have the Product Manager run the projects for a period of time.

Also if you have standard role definitions, and want to change the lines between you, you should if it produces better outcomes. I find having the roles written up allows you to have more explicit conversations about these things. “Hey, it looks like you’re responsible for project reporting. How would you feel about me doing the reporting, or helping you with it?”

Some people like RACI charts

You will find a lot of managers that love RACI charts for this same purpose. I find them inscrutable — they take a lot of attention (for me) to understand.

What is useful about RACI charts is that they define responsibilities in a more nuanced way. So, for example, they make it clear who needs to be informed about certain types of changes.

I generally find them to be more effective when you’re dealing with a lot of parties. Stakeholders and multiple people, all doing similar work. The ownership exercise I share here is simpler, faster, and easier, for when you have less parties involved.

If you are in favor of RACI charts, please be aware that not everyone understands them intuitively. It can be useful to walk through them.

Let me know what you think!

I find this exercise to be a quick way to get clarity and alignment around roles. Try it and let me know what you think!

This post (with additional images) is available at https://www.rubick.com/role-definition/

Thank you

Image courtesy of UK Black Tech. Licensed under the Creative Commons 2.0 license. Image was cropped.

item.title: 我在 github 上的项目。 | My project on github.
item.description:
中文

这是一个用java编写的开源日志框架。 配置文件的名称不受限制。 支持两种类型的配置文件。 在 log4j 中，配置文件只能在 classpath 中，名称只能是 log4j2.xml

初学者无需寻找配置文件示例

以后会增加对toml配置文件的支持

不支持 JDBC <- 2022/6/7，Optilog 可以发送日志到数据库，Optilog 已经支持 jdbc ！！！

由carbon.now.sh生成

欢迎在github上看我的项目~~



原文:

中文

This is an open source logging framework written in java. Optilog has more advantages than other open source logging frameworks (such as log4j and SLF4j)

Optilog advantages

1.High performance: After initialization log, output a log (output to the screen + output to a file + output to the server through socket) only takes 8 milliseconds at most (it will be optimized later), and log4j takes at least 41 milliseconds.

2.Optilog supports unlimited placeholders and can be reused (#1 #1 outputs the first placeholder twice). Log4j only supports 9 placeholders.

3.The configuration file can be placed in any location. The name of the configuration file is unlimited. Two types of configuration files are supported. In log4j, the configuration file can only be in classpath and the name can only be log4j2.xml

4.Support the client to send logs to the server(See details in wiki.Download OptilogServer in OptilogServer)

5.All possible exceptions in optilog are captured without affecting the main logic.

6.You only need one jar package, and you can use it directly by typing in the classpath (I don't know the feeling of adding dozens of jar packages at once when using gradle)

7.Suitable for beginners without complex configuration files

8.There are few dependencies

9.Class, method and other information output in the log is absolutely accurate, because optilog uses stacktrace to determine this information internally, and there is no need to write the class instance when initializing the log

10.Optilog can directly generate a default configuration file. Beginners do not need to look for configuration file examples

11.Support configuration files in multiple formats, including JSON,YAML, XML and properties. Support for toml configuration files will be added later

Defects of optilog

  1. No JDBC support <- On 2022/6/7,Optilog can send log to DataBase,Optilog already support jdbc!!!

Optilog log picture:


Generate by carbon.now.sh

Welcome to see my project on github~~

item.title: 关键词研究的5个步骤 | 5 Steps of Keyword research
item.description:
信息图：关键词研究的 5 个步骤
在您的内容页面或网站或在线页面上拥有许多新的追随者和访问者真是太好了。 这会增加您网页的流量。 增加页面流量的原因之一是为您的内容使用或使用正确的关键字。

关键字是用户通过任何搜索引擎最常搜索的词，它使任何搜索引擎都可以在搜索页面上显示您的页面。

因此，出现在搜索页面上会间接增加您页面的访问量。 最好的和初级的关键字会带来令人愉快的 SEO（搜索引擎优化）活动。

关键字研究是探索关键字并利用它们来增强您的 SEO（搜索引擎优化）活动的过程。 这可以通过许多工具来实现，这些工具将最常用的词放在搜索引擎中进行搜索。

关键字很重要，因为它们会告知搜索引擎您网站或在线页面上的内容。 关键字是用于搜索重要主题以提取信息的词。 关键字是关于用户的。

元描述：

他们是：

  1. 关键字探索或研究

  2. 你写的内容

  3. 链接建设

关键字探索是最重要的功能，可以增加流量。 您可以在博客中拥有最好的内容和链接建设

流量低，因为您没有正确研究和使用关键字。 以有效的方式使用关键字，您可以使您的页面出现在搜索页面的第一页上。

关键字探索的 5 个步骤如下所述：

从一个主题基本关键字开始：
您需要在您的网页或在线页面上收集与您的主题相关且相关的关键字。 您需要明确要撰写的主题以探索关键字。 您可以开始探索一个主题的关键字，而不必担心您拥有的每个主题。 开始缓慢而稳定。 一开始，您一定不要为需要高水平研究或大量关键字研究的主题探索关键字。 您可以从探索关键字的基本主题开始。

您需要清楚用户意图。 用户意图是用户在搜索引擎上搜索查找信息的一种方式。 您还应该清楚用户意图。 这有助于改善您的内容。 您还可以检查用户意图是否针对您的网页或在线页面的业务目标。

着陆页针对或关注网页的用户意图。 探索主题时，您应该牢记着陆页。

种子关键字是您在分析和构思整个主题的关键字时使用的开场关键字。 您需要明确主题的种子关键字。

您需要记住，您的种子关键字是与主题相关并确保用户意图的包容性和综合性关键字。 您将需要利用此种子关键字来查找与该主题相关的确定和特殊关键字。

分析发现更多关键词：
当您分析和发明更多关键字时，您需要以提高数量而不是质量为目标。 如果关键字与用户意图匹配或具有相同的含义，则可以使用它。

在查找用户使用的关键字时，您需要有公平和正直的心态或观点。 您需要确保关键字与用户意图一致。 您需要确保分析的关键字与您想到的目标网页一致。 您可以连接同义词、单数/复数词、形容词等。

您需要注意并感知已经为您的目标网页排名的低处理关键字。 您可以将 Google Search Console 的效果报告用于您的登录页面。 通过这种方式，您可以观察到已经在 Google 的第一页和第二页上排名的关键字。

您可以使用 Google 关键字规划师。 它是一种用于搜索引擎优化的关键字探索工具。 您可以通过在 Google 关键字规划器中填写您的种子关键字来使用它，并且您可以评估 Google 的关键字建议。 如果您找到了一个好的关键字，您可以恢复新关键字，并且您可以经常使用此过程添加新关键字。

了解竞争对手的研究很重要。 在这里 Ubersuggest 可以免费使用。 您可以以低成本使用 SERanking，而 SEMrush 是首选。

您可以使用上述任何竞争对手的工具。 您可以从竞争对手的研究中准备与用户意图相关的最终关键字列表。

您需要过滤掉关键字：
您需要放置正确数量的关键字，这使得目标更容易实现。 您可以升级或排队至少 5-6

关键词。 最多可包含 15-20 个关键字。 确保所有关键字都与用户意图相关。

您需要定位每月搜索量最高的关键字。 要知道搜索量最高的关键字，您需要获取每个关键字的 Google 每月搜索量并加以利用。 您也可以将 Google 关键字规划器用于数量，但只有在您拥有 Google 广告帐户时才能使用它。 因此，如果没有 Google 广告，您可以使用 Ubersuggest 等免费工具。

您需要退出与您的业务相关的对齐关键字较少的关键字。 您需要退出低容量关键字....阅读更多



原文:

Infographic: The 5 Steps of Keyword Research
It’s so good to have many new followership and visitors to your content page or website or online page. This increases the traffic to your webpage. One of the reasons that enhance the traffic to your page is using or utilizing the correct keywords for your content.

Keywords are the words that are most frequently searched by users through any search engine which enables any search engine to show your page on the search page.

So, appearing on the search page indirectly increases the traffic to your page. Finest and primer keywords lead to a delightful SEO (search engine optimization) campaign.

Keyword research is the process of exploring the keywords and utilizing them to enhance your SEO (search engine optimization) campaign. This can be achieved by numerous tools which give the most frequently used words to search in search engines.

Keywords are important as they inform the search engines about your content on your website or online page. The keyword is the word that is used to search for a significant topic to extract information. The keyword is all about users.

Meta description:

SEO is not so easy, but it can make it easy through 3 preliminaries:

They are:

  1. Keyword exploration or research

  2. Content you write

  3. Link building

Keyword exploration is the most vital feature which can increase traffic. You can have the best content and link building in your blog

with low traffic because you didn’t research the keywords correctly and utilized them. Using keywords in an efficient way you can make your page appear on the first page of the search page.

The 5 steps for keyword exploration are described below:

Start with one topic fundamental keyword:
You need to collect the keywords which are related and relevant to your topic on your web page or online page. You need to be clear on the topic you want to write about to explore keywords. You can start exploring keywords for one single topic rather than worrying about every topic you have. Start slow and steady. In the beginning, you must not explore keywords for the topic that needs high-level research or high-volume keyword research. You can start with a basic topic for exploring keywords.

You need to be clear on the user intent. User intent is the one user searches for finding the information on search engines. You should have clarity on the user intent also. This helps in improving your content. You can also check the user intent is disposed to your webpage or online page business aims.

Landing page targets or focuses on the user intent of the webpage. You should keep the landing page in your mind when exploring a topic.

Seed keywords are the opening keywords you utilize when you analyze and conceive keywords all over the topic. You need to have clarity on the seed keyword on your topic.

You need to keep in mind that your seed keyword is the inclusive and comprehensive keyword that is related to and assures the user intent of the topic. You will need to utilize this seed keyword to find determine and peculiar keyword related to the topic.

Analyze and discover more keywords:
When you analyze and invent more keywords you need to target enhancing the quantity than quality. If the keyword matches or gives the same meaning as user intent, you can utilize it.

You need to have a fair and upright mindset or viewpoint in finding the keywords users use. You need to make sure the keyword aligns with the user intent. You need to ensure that the analyzed keywords align with the landing page you have in mind. You can adjoin synonyms, singular/plural words, adjectives, etc.

You need to notice and perceive the low-handling keywords that have already ranked for your landing page. You can use Google’s Search Console’s performance report for your landing page. By this, you can observe keywords that are already ranked on the first and second pages of Google.

You can use Google keyword planner. It is a kind of tool for keyword exploration for SEO. you can use this by filling out your seed keyword in the Google keyword planner and you can evaluate Google’s keyword recommendations. If you found a good keyword you can restore the new keyword and you can frequently use this process to add new keywords.

It’s important to know about competitor research. Ubersuggest, SERanking, SEMrush discloses or tells us about the competitor research. Here Ubersuggest is free to use. You can use SERanking at a low cost and SEMrush is a premier.

You can use any competitor tool mentioned above. You can prepare a final list of keywords that are relevant to user intent from competitor research.

You need to filter out the keywords:
You need to put the right number of keywords this makes the target easier to achieve. You can level up or line up for a minimum of 5-6

keywords. The maximum number can be up to 15-20 keywords. Make sure all keywords are relevant to user intent.

You need to target the keywords that have the highest monthly search volume. To know the highest volume keywords, you need to get Google’s monthly search volumes for each keyword and utilize them. You can also utilize Google keyword planner for volumes, but you can use this only if you have a Google ads account. So, if don’t have Google ads you can use free tools like Ubersuggest, etc.

You need to quit on the keywords with fewer alignment keywords concerning your business. You need to quit on low-volume keywords....Read more

item.title: React - 清洁架构 - Redux 工具包 | React - Clean Architecture - Redux Toolkit
item.description:
该项目是一个示例代码，用于将 Clean Architecture 引入 Web 服务或同时使用 Redux Architecture 和 Clean Architecture。

如果您有任何建议或意见，请告诉我。

使用堆栈

存储库链接

清洁架构

与各种架构一样，干净架构的主要目的是分离关注点。 根据每个兴趣划分层次结构，设计以领域为中心而不是详细实现，并确保内部区域不依赖于框架或数据库 UI 等外部元素。

  • 区分详细的实施领域和领域领域。
  • 架构不依赖于框架。
  • 外部区域可以依赖于内部区域，但内部区域不能依赖于外部区域。

  • 高级和低级模块都依赖于抽象。

  • 实体：实体封装了业务规则或最通用的高级规则。 实体可以是具有方法的对象，也可以是一组数据结构和函数。 当外部事物发生变化时，它们最不可能发生变化。

  • 用例：用例层包含特定于应用程序的业务规则。 它封装并实现了系统的所有用例。这一层的变化会影响特性。 该层不应受到数据库、用户界面等外部性变化的影响。 用例层与这些关注点隔离开来。 应用程序操作的更改不会影响用例。 如果用例的细节发生变化，那么该层中的某些代码肯定会受到影响。

  • 接口适配器：适配器层是一组适配器，用于将数据从用例格式和实体转换为外部层（如数据库或 Web）的格式。

  • 框架和驱动程序：模型的最外层通常由框架组成，例如 Web 框架数据库。

  • 跨越边界：在图表的右下角，控制器和演示者与下一层的用例进行通信。 控制流：它从控制器开始，通过用例，然后通过演示者运行。 还要注意源代码依赖关系：每个都指向用例。

遵守这些简单的规则并不难，它将创建一个可测试、可维护并具有所有好处的系统。 当系统的外部部件之一过时时，可以在最短的时间内完成更换。

清洁架构实施

在以抽象的方式定义干净架构之后，这是该架构在该图描述的 Web 应用程序上下文中的投影：

  • 实体和用例协同工作，是系统的中心。 与框架第一层的通信是通过接口使用 DataSource 完成的。

  • 有两个主要部分业务和框架，业务部分是系统中最重要的部分，因为它包含独立于其他部分的业务逻辑。

目录结构

详细的架构概念

为了详细解释这个架构，我们举一个插入注释的简单例子。

  • 首先，我们创建一个 Note 实体。

  • 然后我们定义插入注释的用例以及与框架层的交互。

从这个图中可以测试用例而不需要实际的网络或缓存服务，其他部分也是如此。 现在在交互器目录中我们可以看到不同的用例，例如类（图中的 InserNewNote 示例），这有助于设计一个易于维护的系统，并且已经描述了它提高了团队合作的性能，并且它支持并行工作。

通讯流程

我们可以区分两种情况：

  • 没有网络调用的流程：

代码

截屏

学分



原文:

This project is a sample code to introduce a Clean Architecture to a web service or to use a Redux Architecture and a Clean Architecture together.

If you have any suggestion or comments lets me know.

Use Stack

Typescript, Webpack, React, Redux-toolkit.

Repository link

https://github.com/Abouelyatim/React-App-Clean-Architecture

Clean Architecture

As with various architectures, the primary purpose of a clean architecture is to separate concerns. Divide the hierarchy according to each interest, design domain-centric rather than detailed implementation, and make sure that the internal area does not depend on external elements such as the framework or database UI.

  • Distinguish between detailed implementation areas and domain areas.
  • Architecture does not depend on the framework.
  • The outer zone can depend on the inner zone, but the inner zone cannot depend on the outer zone.

  • Both high-level and low-level modules rely on abstraction.

  • Entities: Entities encapsulate business rules or the most general, high-level rules. An entity can be an object with methods or a set of data structures and functions. They are the least likely to change when something external changes.

  • Use Cases: The use case layer contains application-specific business rules. It encapsulates and implements all use cases of the system.Changes in this layer affect features. This layer should not be affected by changes to externalities such as database, user interface. The use case layer is isolated from these concerns. Changes to the operation of the application do not affect use cases. If the details of a use case change, then some code in that layer will certainly be affected.

  • Interface Adapters: The adapter layer is a set of adapters that convert data from use case format and entities, to format for an external layers such as database or web.

  • Frameworks and Drivers: The outermost layer of the model is usually made up of frameworks such as the web framework database.

  • Crossing Boundaries: At the bottom right of the diagram, the controller and presenters communicating with the use cases in the next layer. The control flow: it starts in the controller, moves through the use case, and then runs through the presenter. Also note the source code dependencies: each points to the use case.

Complying with these simple rules is not difficult, and it will create a system that is testable, maintainable, and with all the benefits that entails. When one of the external parts of the system becomes obsolete, the replacement is done in a minimum of time.

Clean Architecture Implementation

After the definition of clean architecture in an abstract way, here is the projection of this architecture in the context of a web app described by this diagram:

  • Entities and use cases work together and are the center of the system. Communication with the first layer of the Framework is done with DataSource via an interface.

  • There are two main parts Business and Framework, the business part is the most important part in the system because it contains the business logic independently of the other parts.

Directory Structure

Detailed Architecture Concepts

To explain this architecture in detail, we take a simple example of inserting a note.

  • First,we create a Note entity.

  • Then we define the use case of inserting a note and the interaction with the framework layer.

From this figure it is possible to test the use cases without needing the actual network or cache service, and the same for the other parts. Also now in the interactors directory we can see the different use cases like classes (InserNewNote example in the figure) this helps to design a system that is easy to maintain, and has described itself what it improves the performance of the teamwork ,and it supports parallel work.

Communication Flow

We can distinguish two cases:

  • Flow without network call:

1- Click event on the ui.

2- Action dispatched to the store.

3- Find correspond reducer with action.

4- Get current state.

5- Update state.

6- Notify ui to update.

1- Click event on the ui.

2- Action dispatched to the store.

3- Dispatch use case (thunk).

4- Request api.

5- Response from api.

6- Find correspond reducer with action.

7- Get current state.

8- Update state.

9- Notify ui to update.

Code

https://github.com/Abouelyatim/React-App-Clean-Architecture
